footer.tagline: Standing up for pawnshop customers across Malaysia
footer.disclaimer: This is a public advocacy site. All personal information has been anonymized. WELLCHIP is a publicly listed company (Bursa: 5325).

home.heroTitle: They Cheated a Customer. We Documented It.
home.heroSubtitle: A Bursa-listed pawnshop chain charged above the legal interest rate — and above the rate on their own ticket. We have the receipts. Now we're helping everyone else fight back.
home.caseSummary: In December 2025, Mr. C pawned a gold bracelet at a WELLCHIP (大元當) branch in Johor Bahru for RM 4,000. The pawn ticket was clear: 1% first month, 1.5% after that — RM 40.50 per month. Instead, the shop charged RM 100 every month. That's 2.5% — above their own ticket rate and above the 2% legal cap under the Pawnbrokers Act 1972. No warning. No explanation. Just a higher number on the app.
home.caseAdmission: When confronted, the shop told Mr. C they "no longer follow the receipt given." That's not a policy dispute. That's an admission.
home.casePoliceReport: Police report LARKIN/006020/26 was filed on 1 May 2026.
home.whyTitle: Why This Is Bigger Than One Customer
home.why1Desc: WELLCHIP (stock code 5325) trades on Bursa Malaysia and runs 27+ branches. This isn't a dodgy back-alley shop. It's a publicly accountable company that broke the law.
home.why2Desc: The Pawnbrokers Act 1972 sets the ceiling at 2% per month. WELLCHIP charged 2.5%. That's not a rounding error — it's a criminal offence.
home.why3Desc: If this happened to you — at WELLCHIP or any other pawnshop in Malaysia — submit your complaint. Every story adds pressure. Enough pressure gets enforcement.
home.complaintsDesc: You're probably not the only one. If a pawnshop in Malaysia charged you more than the ticket said, we want to hear it. Submit your story and we'll add it to the public record.

story.title: Here's What Actually Happened
story.subtitle: A routine gold pawn. A clear ticket. And a shop that decided the ticket didn't apply to them anymore.
story.t1Desc: Mr. C brings a gold bracelet (9.1g, 2 pieces) to the WELLCHIP Larkin branch and receives RM 4,000. Ticket YC/0695 is black and white: 1% first month, 1.5% every month after. That works out to RM 40.50 per month. Simple.
story.t2Desc: Month 2. The app shows RM 100.50 — not RM 40.50. That's RM 60 extra. Mr. C questions it. The owner shrugs and says he "cannot do anything." Just follow the app.
story.t3Desc: This time: RM 40.50. The correct amount. No explanation for the previous overcharge.
story.t4Desc: RM 40.50 again, paid via Touch 'n Go. Correct.
story.t5Desc: Mr. C goes in to redeem his bracelet. The shop charges RM 100 again — RM 59.50 more than the ticket rate. And this time, they say it out loud: "We no longer follow the receipt given." A witness heard it.
story.t6Desc: Police report LARKIN/006020/26 filed at Balai Polis Larkin. The report names the overcharges, the amounts, and the shop's own admission.
story.evidenceSubtitle: Documents from the actual case. Personal details have been redacted.
story.ev3Desc: App screenshot showing RM 100.50 charged on 6 Feb 2026
story.ev4Desc: App showing RM 100 interest payable — above both the ticket rate and the legal limit
story.ev5Desc: The WELLCHIP pawn ticket itself — 1%/1.5% rate, RM 4,000 loan, right there in print
story.v1: Charged rate of 2.5% exceeds the 2% legal cap under Section 17 of the Pawnbrokers Act 1972
story.v2: Charged rate also exceeds the rate they printed on their own pawn ticket (1%/1.5%)
story.v3: The shop's statement — "no longer follow the receipt" — is a deliberate, admitted breach
story.v4: Possible criminal offence under Section 420 of the Penal Code (cheating)

rights.title: Know Your Rights
rights.subtitle: The law is on your side. Here's what the Pawnbrokers Act 1972 actually says.
rights.interestDesc: Under Section 17 of the Pawnbrokers Act 1972 and the 2004 Regulations, no pawnbroker can charge you more than:
rights.interestNote: And here's the part most people don't know: if your pawn ticket shows a lower rate — say 1% or 1.5% — the shop is legally bound to that rate. Charging more is a breach of contract AND a criminal offence. Both at once.
rights.r1: Right to a pawn ticket that clearly states the loan amount, interest rate, and redemption terms
rights.r2: Right to at least a 3-month extension if you can't redeem by the deadline
rights.r3: Right to have your jewellery accurately weighed
rights.r4: Right to any surplus if your unredeemed item is auctioned — and they must notify you within 7 days
rights.r5: Right to inspect records within 4 months of any auction
rights.r6: Right to compensation of loan amount plus 25% if your item is lost or damaged
rights.r7: Right to a free copy of records if you lose your pawn ticket
rights.penaltiesTitle: What Happens to Shops That Break the Rules
rights.channel1Desc: Go to your nearest police station. Bring everything: pawn ticket, payment receipts, IC, photos, and a written timeline. For fraud cases, go in person — don't just call.
rights.channel2Desc: KPKT licenses pawnbrokers. They can suspend or revoke a shop's license and order restitution. File via the SISPAA portal at kpkt.spab.gov.my, email bkkk@kpkt.gov.my, or call 03-8000 8000.
rights.channel3Desc: If the shop ignores your demand letter, take it to the Consumer Tribunal. Filing costs just RM 5. Their decisions are legally binding — the shop can't just ignore it.
rights.channel4Desc: Call 03-7877 9000 or go to ncccaduan.firebaseapp.com. NCCC can mediate and escalate directly to KPKT or PDRM if needed.

complaints.title: Public Complaints
complaints.subtitle: Real stories from real people, across Malaysia. Every complaint is reviewed before it goes public.
complaints.noComplaints: No verified complaints yet. If this happened to you, be the first to say so.
complaints.submitSubtitle: If a pawnshop in Malaysia charged you more than they should have, say so here. We review every complaint before publishing — your story will be verified, not just posted.
complaints.formDescriptionHint: Be specific: what rate was agreed, what did they actually charge, and what did they say when you pushed back?
complaints.formEvidenceHint: Pawn tickets, receipts, app screenshots — anything you have. Max 5 MB per file.
complaints.formSuccess: Got it. Your complaint has been submitted and is now in review. We'll publish it once verified.
complaints.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
complaints.formPrivacy: Your phone number and email stay private — always. Only your name, the pawnshop details, and your description will be shown publicly, and only after we've reviewed everything.

about.title: About PawnJustice
about.subtitle: We're not lawyers or NGOs. We're customers who got fed up.
about.whoDesc: PawnJustice started when ordinary Malaysians discovered that a Bursa-listed pawnshop chain was quietly overcharging customers — collecting more than their own tickets promised, and more than the law allows. We documented it, filed a police report, and built this site so others could do the same.
about.mission1: Put documented overcharging cases on the public record
about.mission2: Help people understand what the Pawnbrokers Act 1972 actually says — in plain language
about.mission3: Build a body of complaints large enough that regulators can't ignore it
about.mission4: Push KPKT to do its job and actually enforce the rules against licensed pawnbrokers
about.transparencyDesc: Everything on this site is based on real events with real documents. Names are anonymized to protect individuals. WELLCHIP is named by name because it's a publicly listed company (Bursa: 5325) — their conduct is a matter of public interest, not a private dispute. Evidence photos have personal details redacted.
about.contactDesc: Media inquiries, offers of legal support, or questions about this site — use the complaint form or reach out through the channels on the Your Rights page.

news.subtitle: Pawnshop overcharging isn't just our story. Here's what's happening across Malaysia.

guide.title: Step-by-Step: How to File Your Complaint
guide.subtitle: Don't know where to start? We've broken it down for you — every step, every form, every phone number.
guide.policeIntro: A police report creates an official record. Even if police don't investigate immediately, the report number is crucial for everything else.
guide.policeStep1: Go to your nearest balai polis (police station). For JB: Balai Polis Larkin or IPD Johor Bahru Utara.
guide.policeStep2: Tell the front desk you want to lodge a report about fraud / cheating (penipuan) by a pawnbroker. Ask for the Criminal Investigation Division (CID) counter.
guide.policeStep3: Bring: your IC (original), pawn ticket (original + copy), all payment receipts or app screenshots, any photos of the shop, and a written timeline.
guide.policeStep4: In your statement, clearly state: the pawn ticket rate vs. the actual amount charged, specific dates and amounts of overcharging, and the shop's response when confronted.
guide.policeStep5: Key BM phrases for your report: "Saya telah dikenakan caj faedah melebihi kadar yang tertera pada tiket pajak" (I was charged interest exceeding the rate on my pawn ticket).
guide.policeStep6: Get your report copy immediately. Note the report number (format: STATION/NUMBER/YEAR) and the Investigating Officer's name and phone.
guide.policeTip: Pro tip: If the police say this is a "civil matter" and refuse, politely insist it's a criminal matter under Section 420 Penal Code (cheating). You have the right to lodge a report.
guide.kpktIntro: KPKT is the ministry that licenses pawnbrokers. They have the power to suspend or revoke licenses — this is the lever that scares pawnshop owners most.
guide.kpktStep1: Go to https://kpkt.spab.gov.my (SISPAA portal) and register an account if you don't have one.
guide.kpktStep2: Click "Buat Aduan Baru" (New Complaint). Select category: Pajak Gadai / Pawnbroker.
guide.kpktStep3: Fill in: your details, the pawnshop name and address, a description of the overcharging with dates and amounts.
guide.kpktStep4: Upload attachments: pawn ticket photo, payment receipts, police report copy.
guide.kpktStep5: Submit and save your reference number. KPKT typically responds within 15 working days.
guide.kpktStep6: Alternative: Email bkkk@kpkt.gov.my with all documents attached, or call 03-8000 8000 during office hours.
guide.kpktStep7: What KPKT can do: investigate, issue fines, suspend/revoke the pawnbroker's license, and order them to refund your money.
guide.demandIntro: A formal demand letter puts the pawnshop on notice. It's also required before you can file with the Consumer Tribunal.
guide.demandStep1: Write a formal letter stating: who you are, what happened (dates, amounts), the laws violated (Pawnbrokers Act s.17), and what you want (refund of overcharged amount).
guide.demandStep3: Send by registered post (Pos Berdaftar) — keep the posting receipt as proof.
guide.demandStep4: Or download our ready-made template below and fill it in.
guide.demandTip: You can also hand-deliver and get them to sign an acknowledgment copy.
guide.tribunalIntro: If the pawnshop ignores your demand letter after 14 days, go to the Tribunal. It's fast, cheap, and legally binding.
guide.tribunalStep1: Go to the nearest Tribunal Tuntutan Pengguna office. Find locations at ttpm.kpdn.gov.my.
guide.tribunalStep3: Fill out Form 1 (Borang 1) — claim form. State the amount you're claiming (max RM 50,000).
guide.tribunalStep4: Attach: demand letter + postal receipt, pawn ticket, payment evidence, police report, KPKT complaint receipt.
guide.tribunalStep5: A hearing date will be set (usually within 60 days). You represent yourself — no lawyer needed.
guide.tribunalStep6: The tribunal's decision is LEGALLY BINDING. If the pawnshop doesn't comply, it's contempt of court.
guide.ncccIntro: The National Consumer Complaints Centre can mediate and escalate your case.
guide.ncccStep3: NCCC will mediate between you and the pawnshop and can escalate to KPKT or PDRM if needed.
guide.downloadDesc: Ready-to-print complaint letter. Fill it in, attach your evidence, and send it.
